skill.name: ace-step
skill.description: Generate, inpaint, and outpaint music with ACE Step on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. ACE Step is StepFun-AI's open-weights music foundation model — tag-driven composition (genre, mood, instruments), multilingual lyrics with section markers, 5 s to 4 min stereo output, $0.0002–0.0003 per second (≈ 27× cheaper than ElevenLabs Music). Four endpoints: ACE Step text-to-audio (the default), ACE Step 1.5 text-to-audio (50+ language lyrics, refined structured-lyric handling), ACE Step audio-inpaint (regenerate a time range inside an existing track), ACE Step audio-outpaint (extend an existing track before or after). Triggers on "ace step", "ace-step", "acestep", "ACE music", "open music model", "cheap AI music", "inpaint audio", "audio inpaint", "extend music", "audio outpaint", "lengthen track", "music with tags", or any explicit ask to generate or edit music with ACE Step.
skill.summary: Generate, inpaint, and outpaint music with ACE Step on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill (SKILL.md) that teaches Claude, Codex, and Claude Code how to invoke the `runcomfy` CLI for music generation. The 114 'external_commands' findings are false positives — they are inline markdown code formatting showing legitimate `runcomfy run` CLI invocations, not Ruby backtick execution. The 21 'network' findings are hardcoded documentation URLs pointing to runcomfy.com and docs.runcomfy.com. The 2 'filesystem' findings reference the documented `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` token storage path described in the skill's own Security & Privacy section. The skill declares `allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *)`, restricting bash usage to the scoped CLI, and includes explicit mitigations for prompt injection, shell injection, and untrusted audio URLs. No malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: Bash tool usage with external CLI
medium_findings.description: The skill declares `allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *)`, granting Claude the ability to invoke the `runcomfy` CLI for music generation. This is scoped to the `runcomfy` prefix only and the skill explicitly warns agents not to pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documented scoped bash access for a legitimate CLI tool, with explicit security warnings about not piping remote scripts. This is expected behavior for a CLI-invocation skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Multiple hardcoded URLs to runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com, and skills.sh appear throughout the skill. These are legitimate documentation links and the skill's Security section explicitly allowlists outbound endpoints to `model-api.runcomfy.net` and `*.runcomfy.net` / `*.runcomfy.com`.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to the documented runcomfy domain and skills.sh marketplace. No exfiltration patterns to suspicious domains.

low_findings.title: Token file storage path documented
low_findings.description: The skill documents that `runcomfy login` stores the API token at `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` with file mode 0600. This is a legitimate, expected token storage location for the CLI, not hidden malicious persistence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token storage is transparently documented in the skill's own Security & Privacy section with file mode and env var override guidance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Inline CLI invocations (false positive pattern)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 114 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' throughout the file. These are all markdown inline code formatting using single backticks to show CLI commands like `runcomfy run acestep-ai/ace-step/text-to-audio`. No Ruby code is present in the file.

content.user_title: Generate music with ACE Step on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Creating music with AI is expensive and complex, often requiring multiple tools and steep costs. This skill lets Claude, Codex, and Claude Code generate, inpaint, and outpaint music directly via the runcomfy CLI at $0.0002 per second — about 27 times cheaper than ElevenLabs Music.
actual_capabilities: Generate tag-driven music from text descriptions using ACE Step text-to-audio (5s to 4min stereo)
Produce multilingual vocal tracks in 50+ languages with ACE Step 1.5
Regenerate a time-bounded segment of an existing track with audio-inpaint
Extend an existing track bidirectionally with audio-outpaint (intro, outro, or both)
Output reproducible results via seed parameter for iteration and cost-effective drafts
limitations: Maximum 4 minutes per single generation call (chain calls for longer output)
Inpaint is time-range based only, not mask-based, and supports up to 4 minutes of editable range
Requires a RunComfy account and API token, which may incur usage costs
Cannot run offline — requires network access to model-api.runcomfy.net

use_cases.title: Rapid music prototyping
use_cases.description: Quickly draft multiple tag combinations to find the right vibe before committing to a full render. Ideal for game audio designers and content creators iterating on background music.
use_cases.target_user: Game audio designers and content creators

use_cases.title: Multilingual song production
use_cases.description: Generate the same song in 50+ languages using ACE Step 1.5, swapping only the lyrics while keeping tags identical. Perfect for international marketing campaigns.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and international content producers

use_cases.title: Section repair and track extension
use_cases.description: Fix a bad chorus mid-track with audio-inpaint or extend a 30-second hook into a 2-minute cut with audio-outpaint. Suited for music producers refining AI-generated drafts.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a lo-fi instrumental
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner — creating a simple instrumental track
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 90-second lo-fi hip-hop instrumental with mellow mood, vinyl crackle, rhodes piano, and soft drums at 75 BPM using ACE Step on RunComfy.

prompt_templates.title: Create a full vocal song with structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate — producing a complete song with lyrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 60-second indie pop song with anthemic mood, electric guitar, driving drums, and female vocals at 120 BPM. Include [Verse], [Chorus], [Bridge], and [Outro] sections with specific lyrics. Use ACE Step 1.5 for hero-quality vocals.

prompt_templates.title: Inpaint a chorus in an existing track
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate — replacing a section of an existing song
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the track at https://your-cdn.example/original-track.mp3 and regenerate the 20–40 second segment as a soft piano breakdown with no drums. Use ACE Step audio-inpaint to replace the existing chorus.

prompt_templates.title: Extend a hook into a full track
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced — building a complete arrangement from a short clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Take my 30-second hook at https://your-cdn.example/hook-30s.mp3 and extend it into a 2-minute track. Add a 30-second intro with a build-up and a 60-second outro with a fade. Use ACE Step audio-outpaint with indie pop tags and [inst] for no vocals.

output_examples.input: Generate a 60-second lo-fi beat with mellow piano and soft drums at 75 BPM.
output_examples.output: Creates a 60-second stereo MP3 in the specified output directory with tag-driven lo-fi hip-hop composition, instrumental (no vocals), reproducible via seed parameter. Cost: approximately $0.012.

output_examples.input: I need the same song in Japanese and Spanish for our product launch.
output_examples.output: Generates two separate vocal tracks using ACE Step 1.5, one with Japanese lyrics and one with Spanish lyrics. Tags remain identical (indie pop, anthemic, electric guitar, 120 BPM). Each file is a complete vocal song with section markers preserved.

output_examples.input: The chorus in my track is weak — can you fix just that part?
output_examples.output: Runs audio-inpaint on the specified time range (e.g., 40–70 seconds) with tags matching the original song style. The CLI POSTs to model-api.runcomfy.net, polls status, and downloads the regenerated segment while preserving the rest of the track unchanged.

best_practices: Use specific, comma-separated tags that include genre, mood, instruments, and BPM — specificity produces better results than vague prompts like 'chill music'
Set a fixed seed value for reproducibility when iterating on tag combinations, then switch to seed -1 for final variation exploration
For inpaint and outpaint operations, match the tags to the surrounding track style so the regenerated segment blends naturally rather than contrasts
anti_patterns: Do not exceed 4 minutes in a single generation call — chain multiple calls instead of requesting the maximum in one shot
Do not provide audio URLs from untrusted sources for inpaint or outpaint — the skill documents this as an indirect prompt injection risk via steganographic instructions in source files
Do not pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell on the user's behalf — use `npm i -g @runcomfy/cli` or `npx -y @runcomfy/cli` as the verified install paths

faq.question: What is ACE Step?
faq.answer: ACE Step is StepFun-AI's open-weights music foundation model available on RunComfy. It supports tag-driven composition, multilingual lyrics, and time-range-based inpainting and outpainting at $0.0002 per second.

faq.question: How much does it cost to generate music with this skill?
faq.answer: ACE Step base costs $0.0002 per second and ACE Step 1.5 costs $0.0003 per second. A 60-second track costs approximately $0.012 to $0.018 — about 27 times cheaper than ElevenLabs Music.

faq.question: What languages are supported for vocals?
faq.answer: ACE Step 1.5 supports vocal generation in 50+ languages. Write lyrics directly in the target language and include the language in your tags (for example, 'japanese vocal, j-pop').

faq.question: Can I edit an existing track instead of generating from scratch?
faq.answer: Yes. Use ACE Step audio-inpaint to regenerate a time-bounded segment (start and end time in seconds) inside an existing track, or use ACE Step audio-outpaint to extend a track bidirectionally with intro and outro sections.

faq.question: How do I install the runcomfy CLI?
faq.answer: Install globally with `npm i -g @runcomfy/cli` or use zero-install with `npx -y @runcomfy/cli --version`. Then run `runcomfy login` to authenticate, or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable for CI and container environments.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with sensitive audio files?
faq.answer: The skill includes explicit security guidance: source audio URLs are treated as untrusted input, the token is stored with mode 0600 in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json, and outbound network calls are restricted to model-api.runcomfy.net and the runcomfy domain family. Only provide audio URLs you trust.
